note: VNM Simulation JSC, Hanoi, Vietnam. RaceSims is an authorized distributor.

vnm_ui.name: VNM User Interface (UI)
notes: Delete the entire old UI folder before installing a new version
Press F1 after opening to access the built-in manual
For shifters version 1.2 or earlier, use UI version 1.x instead

vnm_flash.name: VNM Flash (Firmware Flasher)
notes: Used to update firmware for ALL VNM products

requires_configuration.rFactor 2: Config file available via VNM GitHub
requires_configuration.EA Sport WRC: Game-specific setup guide available from VNM
requires_configuration.BeamNG: Must enable FFB in game: Settings > Force Feedback > Enable FFB

symptoms: Device not appearing in VNM UI
solutions: Try a different USB port — avoid USB hubs, use a direct motherboard port
Check if the USB cable is fully seated on both ends
Open Device Manager and look for unknown devices — right-click and update driver
Download and install the latest VNM UI (v6.1.3.3) which includes drivers
Try a different USB cable — the included USB A-to-B (wheelbase) or USB A-to-C (wheel) cable
Restart the PC after connecting the device
On some motherboards, USB 2.0 ports work more reliably than USB 3.0 for game controllers
troubleshooting.escalation: If none of these work, the controller board may need firmware reflash or replacement — contact RaceSims support

troubleshooting.issue: VNM device not recognized after PC startup
symptoms: Device works when first plugged in but disappears after reboot
Have to unplug and replug after every boot
solutions: This is usually a Windows USB power management issue
Go to Device Manager > Universal Serial Bus controllers > right-click each USB Root Hub > Properties > Power Management > uncheck 'Allow the computer to turn off this device to save power'
Also check: Windows Settings > System > Power & Sleep > Additional power settings > Change plan settings > Change advanced power settings > USB settings > USB selective suspend = Disabled
Try plugging into a USB 2.0 port instead of USB 3.0
Update your motherboard's USB controller drivers from the manufacturer website
As a last resort, create a Windows Task Scheduler task that runs on login to reset the USB device

symptoms: VNM Flash can't find device in DFU mode
solutions: Make sure you've installed the DFU driver — follow VNM's installation manual
The device must be in DFU mode before VNM Flash can see it
Install WinUSB driver using Zadig if the DFU device shows up as an unknown device
Download the DFU driver config from: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vnmsimulation/flasher/main/INSTALL%20VNM%20FLASH%20AND%20CONFIG%20DRIVER%20DFU.pdf
Try different USB ports — DFU mode can be picky about USB 3.0 vs 2.0

troubleshooting.issue: Wheelbase force feedback feels weak or inconsistent
solutions: Check power supply — the DD Xtreme 32Nm requires the included power adapter, not a generic one
In VNM UI, check the FFB strength settings — there are 11 game effects + 4 user effects, each adjustable
Adjust smoothness settings: overall, damper, inertia, and friction can all be tuned
Enable crash effect reduction if FFB spikes are the issue
Check in-game FFB settings — some games default to low FFB strength
For iRacing: use the auto-setup feature, then fine-tune from there
Make sure you're running the latest firmware via VNM Flash

symptoms: Pedal travel not using full range
Brake requires too much or too little force
Dead zones at top or bottom of travel
solutions: Open VNM UI and go to the pedal calibration section
Use the smart calibration software to set deadzones and travel curves
For the brake (200kg load cell): you can adjust the force curve — most people don't need to press to full 200kg
For throttle/clutch (contactless angle sensors): recalibrate if the sensors seem off
Make sure all pedal mounting bolts are tight — loose pedals can cause sensor drift
Check that the controller box USB cable is secure
Check VNM UI to verify the shifter is detected and showing input
For H-pattern: make sure the correct gear plate is installed (4-gear, 5-gear, 7-gear, or 8-gear)
For sequential: check shift force adjustment (adjustable 3.9kg to 5.0kg on SEQ V2)
Verify the shift is being registered in VNM UI before checking in-game settings
In-game: make sure shifter is mapped correctly in the game's controller settings
Non-contact sensors should not wear out — if issues persist, check firmware version

troubleshooting.issue: Handbrake not working or poor feel
solutions: Check VNM UI calibration for the handbrake
The V1.5 Rally version has adjustable handle length and angle (0/15/30/45/60/75/90 degrees) — experiment with angle
Customize stiffness using the included springs — swap to lighter/heavier springs as needed
200kg industrial-grade load cell with 16-bit ADC should be very responsive — if it's not, check USB connection
Make sure the handbrake is mapped in-game (some games require manual mapping)
The GT Steering Wheel uses a GX12-4 to USB coiled cable — check both connections
Open VNM UI and check if button inputs register when pressed
If using with the DD Xtreme, check the USB A-to-C cable connection between base and wheel
Download the Quick Start Guide and User Manual PDFs from VNM website for button mapping
8 LED buttons are configurable with stickers — make sure you're pressing the right ones for your game mapping

dd_xtreme.name: VNM Direct Drive Xtreme
dd_xtreme.motor: Customized smooth industrial servo, 130x130x231mm, 5 pole pairs
dd_xtreme.ffb_features: 11 game effects + 4 user effects, adjustable smoothness for overall/damper/inertia/friction, crash effect reduction, telemetry FFB (experimental)
dd_xtreme.includes: Wheelbase, USB cables, EMC Stop Button, Quick Release (base-side), power adapter
dd_xtreme.upgrade: Industrial-grade slipring available for steering wheel
dd_xtreme.status: Out of stock at VNM direct — available through RaceSims

pedals_v1_3set.name: VNM Pedals V1 (3-pedal)
pedals_v1_3set.bearings: All moving parts use ball bearings
pedals_v1_3set.controller: High-precision controller box with smart calibration software
pedals_v1_3set.note: Heel plate NOT included ($50 separate). Damper kit available ($150).

pedals_v1_2set.name: VNM Pedals V1 (2-pedal)
pedals_v1_2set.note: Same specs as 3-pedal minus clutch. Heel plate NOT included.

lite_pedals_3set.name: VNM Lite Pedals Set 3
lite_pedals_3set.materials: Powder-coated steel + anodized aluminum (vs full aluminum on V1)
lite_pedals_3set.note: Heel plate NOT included. Budget option without sacrificing performance.

shifter_v1.name: VNM Shifter V1 Basic Bundle
shifter_v1.includes: Shifter base, QR Spherical Knob, QR Cylinder Knob, Sequential V2 plate, 7-gear H-pattern plate (6+R), hex wrench set, USB cable
shifter_v1.note: Mounting clamps and bolts sold separately

seq_shifter_v2.name: VNM SEQ Shifter V2
seq_shifter_v2.gears: Hardened SKD11 with chrome plating (wear/rust prevention)
seq_shifter_v2.mounting: Mounts directly on ALU profile without extra brackets

handbrake_v15.name: VNM Handbrake V1.5 Rally
handbrake_v15.handle: Adjustable length, angle adjustable at 0/15/30/45/60/75/90 degrees

gt_steering_wheel.name: VNM GT Steering Wheel V1
gt_steering_wheel.materials: Full anodized CNC aluminum with silicone ergonomic grip
gt_steering_wheel.controls: 8 LED buttons, 4 rotary encoders with push, 2 thumb encoders, 2 five-way funky switches, 2 shift paddles, 2 analog paddles (dual clutch)

warranty.duration: 12 months from purchase OR 18 months from manufacture, whichever comes first
warranty.covers: Defects in material or workmanship under normal use
not_covered: Any product altered after purchase (voids warranty entirely)
warranty.process_racesims: Contact RaceSims support first. We handle warranty claims for all VNM products sold through us. Remote troubleshooting first, then replacement parts if needed.
warranty.replacement_warranty: Replacement parts assume remaining original warranty. If issue occurs in final 30 days, warranty extends 90 additional days.

support_channels.racesims_note: For Indian customers: contact RaceSims first, we handle all support and warranty locally.
